name: Charente-Maritime (France)
description: Per pale: at the hoist, a blue field with a white bishop's mitre over crossed staffs between three gold fleurs-de-lis; at the fly, a red field with a crowned gold cockerel.
history: The mitre and fleurs-de-lis recall the historic provinces of Aunis and Saintonge, while the cockerel is a traditional French emblem.

features.role: per pale, blue at hoist and red at fly
three gold fleurs-de-lis around a white mitre on the blue half
crowned gold cockerel on the red half
